milestone: Save Failed self-heal + header wrap fix
slice: A real, user-screenshotted bug: the top-bar "Save Failed - try a smaller image" badge (AV-009) appeared, and its long text visually overlapped/clipped the Scene Configuration glance sitting beside it in the same header row -- two related findings, both fixed. (1) Root cause of "why does this error come now": _persist()'s local ProjectStore.save() writes the ENTIRE array of every World Project stored under this browser's one 'vihu-world-builder-projects' localStorage key, not just the current Project -- so a save can fail on quota even when the currently-open Project's own images are already externalized, if this Project (or a sibling Project sharing that array) still carries legacy embedded base64 from before the Draft Asset Architecture rewiring shipped. A real, confirmed gap made this worse than a one-off failure: _scheduleAssetMigration() (Phase D) only ever ran as a side effect of an ALREADY-successful save, so a Project already over quota could get permanently stuck -- every edit fails to save for the same reason, and migration (the one thing that would shrink it back under quota) never got a chance to run. Fixed with a new _attemptQuotaSelfHeal(project), called from _persist()'s error branch: it runs the identical migrateFieldsOnSave migration immediately (put() only ever touches local IndexedDB, never a network round trip, so this is safe even offline) against whatever just failed to save, then retries the save once -- silently flipping the badge to "All Changes Saved" the moment migration frees enough room. A Project with nothing left of its own to migrate (the failure came from a different, still-bloated sibling Project) simply fails the retry too, and "Save Failed" stays showing exactly as honestly as before -- disclosed, not silently papered over. (2) The header overlap itself: .wb-workspace-header-left (a flex row holding the World name, Scene Configuration glance, local-save badge, and Cloud Sync badge) had no flex-wrap, and the save-error text has no min-width:0/overflow:hidden of its own (unlike the World name/breadcrumb, which already truncate) -- so a long error message could never shrink and instead pushed the row wider than the header, visually clipping the neighbouring pixel-Size chip. Fixed with flex-wrap:wrap + a small row-gap, the exact same fix already used once for the Welcome screen's Repository-only card badges. Verified via a scratch Playwright check (the CSS wrap renders correctly; a faithful reimplementation of the exact _attemptQuotaSelfHeal control flow -- fail once, migrate, retry, succeed -- confirmed correct against the real algorithm) plus both goldenBuild.js suites, unchanged. No Theme Contract/Engine V2 Scene Model/Builder architecture change; confined to tools/world-builder-v2/js/worldBuilderApp.js and css/world-builder.css.
